benchmark: OmniFuse vs synaptic-memory — extra download-only BEIR/MTEB datasets
note: synaptic ships these only as download scripts (not committed data); fetched from HuggingFace via synaptic tests/benchmark/download_datasets.py. Lexical track: omni field-weighted BM25 vs synaptic FTS-only (run_public_dataset), MRR@10.
correction_2026_07_10: The previously published omnifuse numbers here were measured BEFORE the idf_pow (term-specificity emphasis) change and were never re-run against it — they were stale. Re-measured below under the shipped default idf_pow=1.5, and also at idf_pow=1.0 for reference. Being explicit: the emphasis that wins the core suite REGRESSES the two heavily multi-relevant sets. FURTHER CORRECTION: the claim that emphasis caused BOTH extended losses was wrong. With emphasis off, NFCorpus still lost (0.5080 vs 0.5124) — its cause was missing English morphology, now fixed. Emphasis only widens the MIRACL-ko gap.

datasets.name: NFCorpus
datasets.note: flipped by adding English S-stemmer morphology (was 0.5053)

datasets.name: FiQA
datasets.note: measured pre-idf_pow, NOT re-run; synaptic FTS not measured (57,638-doc SQLite ingest too slow on this 16GB box)

datasets.name: MultiLongDoc-ko
datasets.note: measured pre-idf_pow, NOT re-run; 193MB of long docs, omni capped doc text at 6000 chars (zero-infra RAM bound)

idf_pow_tradeoff.finding: Term-specificity emphasis (idf_pow>1) is a genuine Pareto trade, not a free win. It wins the core suite 10/10 across p in [1.3, 2.0], but on heavily MULTI-RELEVANT passage-IR corpora it hurts: betting the score on one rare term is the wrong strategy when dozens of documents are relevant.
idf_pow_tradeoff.guidance: idf_pow is now a documented knob: build_inmemory(..., vector_kwargs={'idf_pow': 1.0}) for heavily multi-relevant corpora. At p=1.0 MIRACL-ko is 0.9489 vs synaptic 0.9495 — a dead heat — but Ko-StrategyQA then flips to a loss, so 1.5 remains the best single global default (14/15 datasets).
idf_pow_tradeoff.methodology_note: An earlier sweep of this parameter was INVALID: idf_pow is a keyword-only default bound at function-definition time, so monkeypatching the module constant silently changed nothing. The numbers above patch BM25/BM25F.__init__.__kwdefaults__ and the sweep demonstrably varies.

conclusion: After adding English morphology, OmniFuse wins 3 of the 4 extended head-to-heads. MIRACL-ko is the single remaining loss: relevance there is diffuse (14.4 relevant/query) and IDF emphasis — a precision move — costs recall. At idf_pow=1.0 it is a dead heat (0.9489 vs 0.9495) but Ko-StrategyQA then flips, so 1.5 stays the best single global default.
honest_limitations: synaptic's private corpora (krra/assort/x2bee) are gitignored — not in its repo, cannot be run.
FiQA/MultiLongDoc synaptic-FTS not measured (ingest time / RAM on a 16GB shared box); their omnifuse numbers predate idf_pow and were not re-run.
MultiLongDoc omni used a 6000-char doc cap — omnifuse's zero-infra in-memory BM25 cannot hold 193MB of long docs on 16GB RAM; a disk backend would be needed for full text.

english_morphology_2026_07_10.finding: OmniFuse normalized Korean morphology but indexed Latin tokens as raw surface forms — 'statin' never matched 'statins'. This, not the IDF emphasis, was the NFCorpus loss.
english_morphology_2026_07_10.fix: text._en_stem = Harman's S-stemmer (singularize only). No tunable parameter, so nothing to fit.
english_morphology_2026_07_10.rejected_alternative: Augmenting latin tokens with 'surface + #stem' (mirroring the Korean design) scored WORSE than plain replacement: NFCorpus 0.5090 vs 0.5182.
english_morphology_2026_07_10.voided_run: A prior experiment concluded English stemming was harmful. It was invalid: the harness patched omnifuse.text.tokenize, but backends/memory.py binds `tokenize` at import, so DOCUMENTS were indexed with the old tokenizer while QUERIES used the new one. Corrected run reverses the conclusion.

contribution_power_mean_2026_07_10.idea: Raise the precomputed (term,doc) contribution to a power q instead of raising IDF to a power. Ranking by sum(c^q) is a generalized power mean over term evidence, so it emphasizes terms the document ACTUALLY matched well (c carries idf AND tf-saturation), not rare terms it barely touched. Zero query-time cost.
contribution_power_mean_2026_07_10.verdict: NOT shipped. It halves the MIRACL-ko damage but is worse on nine other datasets. Trading nine datasets for two is not an improvement, and picking q per corpus would be the fitting we refuse to do.

miracl_closure_attempts_2026_07_10.conclusion: MIRACL-ko is the single remaining loss and is not closable by tokenization or weighting. Its best score anywhere is 0.9489 (idf_pow=1.0) vs synaptic 0.9495 — a 0.13-query gap. Relevance is diffuse (14.4 relevant/query); IDF emphasis is a precision move, so it trades against Ko-StrategyQA. Structural, not a defect.

variants_measured.idf_pow band 1.0-2.0: MIRACL best 0.9489 at p=1.0, but Ko-StrategyQA then loses
variants_measured.contribution^q power-mean: MIRACL 0.9321 at q=1.2 (core still 8/8) but worse on nine other sets; 12-set avg MRR 0.7628 -> 0.7610
variants_measured.V4 bi-grams over RAW surface + #stem: MIRACL 0.9027; lifts Ko-StrategyQA to 0.6599 and KLUE to 0.8314 (both best seen) but costs AutoRAG/PublicHealthQA/Allganize-Eval; 7-set avg 0.8355 -> 0.8311

miracl_closure_attempts_2026_07_10.refuted_hypothesis: We suspected Hangul DOUBLE EMISSION (overlapping bi-grams + #stem) made bi-gram rarity a segmentation artifact that emphasis amplified. Refuted: removing either emission makes MIRACL WORSE (0.8899 / 0.8968 vs 0.9052).
